evidence: No @Deprecated symbols found anywhere in production code — all deprecated patterns have been actively removed.

Model reorganisation is complete: RelationType and PosGroup live in model/, ExploreOptions/Edge/RelationEdgeType in exploration/, LongIntHashMap in collections/, GrammarConfig extracted from GrammarConfigLoader.

GrammarConfigLoader.posGroupFromConstraint() retains a 'tag=' fallback path alongside the canonical 'xpos=' path to stay compatible with older grammar files; a comment explicitly documents this as intentional backward-compatibility, not an abandoned migration.

RelationConfig.getFullPattern() delegates to substituteHeadword() which itself delegates to CqlUtils.substituteAtPosition() — both methods coexist, but they are a clean chain rather than a stale shim.
ExploreOptions Javadoc explicitly documents the 'Explore' vs 'Exploration' naming asymmetry as intentional, not an incomplete rename.

SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator each contain a TODO comment noting that NOUN_CQL_CONSTRAINT / ADJECTIVE_PATTERN hardcode Penn-Treebank xpos tags and should eventually be derived from RelationConfig; these are tracked, forward-looking items, not stale legacy stubs.
incomplete_migration.issues_preventing_higher_score: The tag=/xpos= dual-path in RelationConfig.posGroupFromConstraint() is intentional but the comment only says 'legacy grammar files'; there is no active test that verifies the fallback still works, so the line between maintained shim and dead code is unclear. The two TODOs in SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator for config-injection are acknowledged but untracked, making it slightly ambiguous when they will be addressed.

strengths: The recent model-reorg is complete: all relocated types (RelationType, PosGroup, ExploreOptions, Edge, RelationEdgeType, LongIntHashMap, GrammarConfig) have been moved and there are no stale import sites left behind.
No @Deprecated or DEPRECATED-annotated symbols remain in production code — deprecated-but-called code is not a problem here.
The tag=/xpos= backward-compat fallback in posGroupFromConstraint() is clearly documented with an inline comment explaining its purpose.
Package-info.java in model/ documents the naming-convention asymmetry between record and non-record types, preventing confusion about mixed styles.
incomplete_migration.issue_character: The only residual migration concerns are two acknowledged TODOs (config-injection for hardcoded POS patterns) and a minimally-documented legacy fallback path — all are small, isolated, and intentional rather than systemic neglect.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: The codebase scores solidly above the 80 ('solid but uneven') anchor. The major structural migration (package reorg) is finished and clean. The remaining items are two forward-looking TODOs that are correctly scoped and a tested-but-not-unit-tested compatibility shim; neither constitutes abandoned migration work. The score stops short of 95 because the two TODOs lack tracking tickets or target dates, leaving a small ambiguity about their lifecycle.

issues.summary: Hardcoded POS constraints in SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator lack tracking and config-injection path
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.NOUN_CQL_CONSTRAINT = "[xpos=\"NN.*\"]"; TODO comment says it should be derived from RelationConfig but requires threading grammar config into this class
issues.suggestion: Pass a GrammarConfig (or a PosGroup→pattern resolver) into SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator via constructor injection. This removes the hardcoded Penn-Treebank assumptions and completes the config-driven architecture that the rest of the code already uses. Track as a discrete task so the TODO lifecycle is visible.

root_causes: The model-reorg was executed as a batch refactor, which cleanly moved types but deferred the config-injection work that would have required deeper changes to SemanticFieldExplorer.
possible_false_positives: tag=/xpos= dual-path in RelationConfig.posGroupFromConstraint() — this is intentional backward-compat, not an incomplete migration
